title: malty molten jamaican pork tagine
description: A rich, comforting Jamaican dish featuring tender pork chops in a malty, molten sauce made with kombu-cold-extract and a blend of spices.
instructions: Heat coconut oil in the bottom of a 4-quart Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is browned and caramelized, about 20 minutes.
Add the kombu pieces and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they start to break down.
Add the allspice, thyme, cinnamon, and 1/4 teaspoon of kosher salt. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the spices are fragrant.
Add the pork chops and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they are browned on both sides.
Pour in the molasses, apple cider vinegar, and 1 cup of water. Bring to a boil, then cover and transfer to the oven.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the pork is tender and the sauce is thick and malty.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
